        (try { simp_all [Fintype.piFinset, Finset.mem_coe, Set.mem_setOf_eq] }) <;>
        (try { ring_nf at * <;> aesop })

        <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try { simp_all [hx'] }) <;>
        (try { ring_nf at * <;> aesop })

intro x hx
      -- Extract the conditions from hx
      simp only [Set.mem_pi, Set.mem_univ, true_and_iff, Prod.mk.injEq] at hx
      -- Show that x squared is in the product of the F_i and A
      have h1 : ∀ i, x i ∈ A i := hx
      have h2 : ∀ i, x i ∈ A i := hx
      -- Use the given condition for each A_i to show x^2 is in F_i • A_i
      have h3 : ∀ i, x i * x i ∈ (F i : Set (G i)) • A i := by
        intro i
        have h4 : x i ∈ A i := h1 i
        have h5 : A i ^ 2 ⊆ (F i : Set (G i)) • A i := hFS i
        have h6 : x i * x i ∈ A i ^ 2 := by
          -- Prove that x_i * x_i is in A_i^2
          simp only [Set.mem_prod, Set.mem_univ, true_and_iff] at hx ⊢
          exact ⟨x i, x i, hx i, hx i⟩
        -- Use the subset condition to conclude the proof
        have h7 : x i * x i ∈ (F i : Set (G i)) • A i := h5 h6
        exact h7
      -- Combine the results to show x^2 is in the product of F_i • A_i
      simp only [Set.mem_pi, Set.mem_univ, true_and_iff, Set.mem_smul, Finset.prod_eq_one] at h3 ⊢
      -- Use the fact that the product of F_i is finite to conclude the proof
      refine' ⟨fun i ↦ ?_, _⟩

        have h8 : x i * x i ∈ (F i : Set (G i)) • A i := h3 i

        rcases h8 with ⟨f, hf, a, ha, rfl⟩
